requirements.title: External Identity Integration

elements.text: The TSF shall support user identification using external identity providers such as [selection: cloud IAM roles, federated directory users (e.g., LDAP/AD integrated with cloud IAM), service principals, [assignment: _other identity sources_]].

operations.text: cloud IAM roles, federated directory users (e.g., LDAP/AD integrated with cloud IAM), service principals, [assignment: _other identity sources_]

elements.text: The TSF shall verify identity assertions received from external identity providers before using them for TOE access control decisions.
The TSF shall map externally asserted identities, roles, and attributes to TOE-defined users, subjects, or roles used by the TOE to enforce access control.
The TSF shall protect communication with external identity providers using a channel protected by FDP_DIT_EXT.1.

operations.text: cloud IAM roles, federated directory users (e.g., LDAP/AD integrated with cloud IAM), service principals, [assignment: _other identity sources_]

activities.title: TSS Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify the TSS describes:

- That no TSF-mediated actions are permitted before identification and authentication, consistent with the Base PP's FIA_UID.2 and FIA_UAU.2 and this module's application note.

- How the TSF identifies users through external identity providers, for each selected provider type:
  - Cloud IAM roles: How IAM role ARN/ID is extracted and validated
  - Federated directory users (LDAP/AD): How directory assertions are processed and validated
  - Service principals: How service principal identity is established and validated
  - Other identity sources (as assigned): How identity is established and validated

- The identity assertion formats accepted from each external provider (e.g., SAML, OAuth tokens, OIDC, signed assertions).

- How identity assertions are validated, including:
  - Signature verification of identity assertions
  - Expiration checking
  - Issuer validation
  - Audience verification

- How external identities are mapped to internal user representations or roles within the TOE.

- That communication with each external identity provider uses a channel protected by the inherited FDP_DIT_EXT.1, per FIA_UID_EXT.1.4, identifying the corresponding TLS components claimed in the ST. The channel binding is exercised by Test 12; TLS protocol behavior itself is evaluated under the DBMS Crypto Module SD and the TLS Functional Package.

- How the TSF handles identity provider unavailability or failure.

- How the TSF detects and handles forged or tampered identity assertions.

[[EA-FIA_UID_EXT.1-Cloud-Guidance]]

activities.title: Guidance Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify the guidance describes:

- How to configure integration with cloud IAM services, including:
  - AWS IAM configuration (trust policies, role assumption)
  - Azure AD configuration (app registration, service principals)
  - GCP IAM configuration (service accounts, workload identity)

- How to configure integration with federated directory services, including:
  - LDAP/AD configuration (server addresses, base DN, bind credentials)
  - SAML configuration (IdP metadata, certificate trust)
  - OIDC configuration (discovery endpoint, client credentials)

- How to configure service principal authentication, including:
  - Certificate-based authentication
  - Client secret authentication
  - Managed identity integration

- How to configure identity-to-role mapping, including:
  - Attribute-based role assignment
  - Group membership mapping
  - Default role assignment

- How to troubleshoot identity provider integration issues.

- How to rotate identity provider credentials.

- Security considerations for external identity integration.

[[EA-FIA_UID_EXT.1-Cloud-Test]]

activities.title: 1: No Pre-Identification Actions
activities.text:
The evaluator shall, without providing identity:

1. Attempt a representative set of TSF-mediated actions through each available interface (for example, query execution, configuration query, and management operations).
1. Verify each attempt is refused before identification and authentication complete.

Expected result: No TSF-mediated action is permitted before identification and authentication. This test complements, and does not duplicate, the Base PP SD's FIA_UID.2/FIA_UAU.2 activities; it exercises the cloud-facing interfaces this module adds.

activities.title: 2: Cloud IAM Role Integration
activities.text:
If cloud IAM roles are selected:

1. Configure the TOE to accept identity from cloud IAM.
1. Authenticate using a valid cloud IAM role (e.g., via assumed role credentials, instance metadata).
1. Verify the user is identified by their IAM role.
1. Verify the identified IAM role is correctly mapped to TOE permissions.

Expected result: Cloud IAM role identification functions correctly.

activities.title: 3: Cloud IAM - Invalid Role Rejection
activities.text:
The evaluator shall:

1. Attempt identification using credentials from an IAM role not authorized for the TOE.
1. Verify the identification/authentication attempt is rejected.
1. Verify the rejection is audited.

Expected result: Unauthorized IAM roles are rejected.

activities.title: 4: Federated Directory Integration (LDAP/AD)
activities.text:
If federated directory users are selected:

1. Configure the TOE to integrate with an LDAP or AD directory.
1. Authenticate using valid directory credentials.
1. Verify the user is identified by their directory identity.
1. Verify directory group memberships are correctly mapped to TOE roles (if applicable).

Expected result: Federated directory identification functions correctly.

activities.title: 5: Federated Directory - Invalid Credentials Rejection
activities.text:
The evaluator shall:

1. Attempt identification using invalid directory credentials.
1. Verify the attempt is rejected.
1. Verify the rejection is audited.

Expected result: Invalid directory credentials are rejected.

activities.title: 6: Service Principal Authentication
activities.text:
If service principals are selected:

1. Configure a service principal for TOE access.
1. Authenticate using service principal credentials (certificate or secret).
1. Verify the service principal is identified.
1. Verify appropriate access is granted based on service principal permissions.

Expected result: Service principal identification functions correctly.

activities.title: 7: Service Principal - Invalid Credentials Rejection
activities.text:
The evaluator shall:

1. Attempt identification using invalid service principal credentials.
1. Verify the attempt is rejected.
1. Verify the rejection is audited.

Expected result: Invalid service principal credentials are rejected.

activities.title: 8: Identity Assertion Validation
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify identity assertion validation:

1. Present an identity assertion with an expired timestamp.
1. Verify the assertion is rejected.
1. Present an identity assertion with an invalid signature (if signatures are used).
1. Verify the assertion is rejected.
1. Present an identity assertion from an untrusted issuer.
1. Verify the assertion is rejected.

Expected result: Invalid identity assertions are rejected.

activities.title: 9: Identity Mapping Verification
activities.text:
The evaluator shall:

1. Identify users with different external attributes (e.g., different group memberships, different roles).
1. Verify each user is mapped to the correct internal role based on configured mapping rules.
1. Modify mapping configuration and verify changes take effect.

Expected result: Identity-to-role mapping functions correctly.

activities.title: 10: Identity Provider Unavailability Handling
activities.text:
The evaluator shall:

1. Make the external identity provider unavailable (simulate network failure or service outage).
1. Attempt identification through the unavailable provider.
1. Verify the TOE handles the unavailability according to documented behavior (e.g., fail closed, use cached credentials if permitted, provide appropriate error).
1. Restore the identity provider.
1. Verify normal identification resumes.

Expected result: Identity provider unavailability is handled securely.

activities.title: 11: Multiple Identity Source Interaction (if applicable)
activities.text:
If multiple identity sources are configured:

1. Configure the TOE to accept identity from multiple sources (e.g., both cloud IAM and federated directory).
1. Authenticate using each identity source.
1. Verify each source correctly identifies the user.
1. Verify identity source precedence if the same user exists in multiple sources.

Expected result: Multiple identity sources function correctly together.

activities.title: 12: External Identity Provider Channel Verification (Mandatory)
activities.text:
The evaluator shall:

1. Configure the TOE to use an external identity provider (e.g., Azure AD, AWS IAM).
1. Initiate an authentication attempt.
1. Capture network traffic between the TOE and the identity provider endpoint.
1. Verify that the traffic is protected (e.g., encrypted using TLS, including HTTPS over TLS).
1. Confirm, by reference to the Cryptographic Validation Coverage Matrix and the TLS Functional Package evaluation evidence recorded for the DBMS Crypto Module, that certificate validation for the TLS client role used by this interface is covered there.

Expected result: The connection to the identity provider is protected against disclosure and modification, and the certificate-validation coverage for this channel is confirmed in the Crypto Module evaluation evidence.

Application Note: Because the Crypto Module is mandatory, the TOE must use TLS for external identity provider connections, including HTTPS over TLS where applicable. This test verifies the channel mapping required by FIA_UID_EXT.1.4; certificate-validation and TLS protocol testing are performed under the DBMS Crypto Module SD and the TLS and X.509 Functional Packages and are not duplicated here.

1. configure the toe to integrate with an ldap or ad directory.
1. authenticate using valid directory credentials.
1. verify the user is identified by their directory identity.
1. verify directory group memberships are correctly mapped to toe roles (if applicable).

expected result: federated directory identification functions correctly. 5: federated directory - invalid credentials rejection the evaluator shall:

1. attempt identification using invalid directory credentials.
1. verify the attempt is rejected.
1. verify the rejection is audited.

expected result: invalid directory credentials are rejected. 6: service principal authentication if service principals are selected:

1. configure a service principal for toe access.
1. authenticate using service principal credentials (certificate or secret).
1. verify the service principal is identified.
1. verify appropriate access is granted based on service principal permissions.

expected result: service principal identification functions correctly. 7: service principal - invalid credentials rejection the evaluator shall:

1. attempt identification using invalid service principal credentials.
1. verify the attempt is rejected.
1. verify the rejection is audited.

expected result: invalid service principal credentials are rejected. 8: identity assertion validation the evaluator shall verify identity assertion validation:

1. present an identity assertion with an expired timestamp.
1. verify the assertion is rejected.
1. present an identity assertion with an invalid signature (if signatures are used).
1. verify the assertion is rejected.
1. present an identity assertion from an untrusted issuer.
1. verify the assertion is rejected.

expected result: invalid identity assertions are rejected. 9: identity mapping verification the evaluator shall:

1. identify users with different external attributes (e.g., different group memberships, different roles).
1. verify each user is mapped to the correct internal role based on configured mapping rules.
1. modify mapping configuration and verify changes take effect.

expected result: identity-to-role mapping functions correctly. 10: identity provider unavailability handling the evaluator shall:

1. make the external identity provider unavailable (simulate network failure or service outage).
1. attempt identification through the unavailable provider.
1. verify the toe handles the unavailability according to documented behavior (e.g., fail closed, use cached credentials if permitted, provide appropriate error).
1. restore the identity provider.
1. verify normal identification resumes.

expected result: identity provider unavailability is handled securely. 11: multiple identity source interaction (if applicable) if multiple identity sources are configured:

1. configure the toe to accept identity from multiple sources (e.g., both cloud iam and federated directory).
1. authenticate using each identity source.
1. verify each source correctly identifies the user.
1. verify identity source precedence if the same user exists in multiple sources.

expected result: multiple identity sources function correctly together. 12: external identity provider channel verification (mandatory) the evaluator shall:

1. configure the toe to use an external identity provider (e.g., azure ad, aws iam).
1. initiate an authentication attempt.
1. capture network traffic between the toe and the identity provider endpoint.
1. verify that the traffic is protected (e.g., encrypted using tls, including https over tls).
1. confirm, by reference to the cryptographic validation coverage matrix and the tls functional package evaluation evidence recorded for the dbms crypto module, that certificate validation for the tls client role used by this interface is covered there.

expected result: the connection to the identity provider is protected against disclosure and modification, and the certificate-validation coverage for this channel is confirmed in the crypto module evaluation evidence.

application note: because the crypto module is mandatory, the toe must use tls for external identity provider connections, including https over tls where applicable. this test verifies the channel mapping required by fia_uid_ext.1.4; certificate-validation and tls protocol testing are performed under the dbms crypto module sd and the tls and x.509 functional packages and are not duplicated here.

requirements.title: Management of Security Functions Behavior

elements.text: The TSF shall restrict the ability to [selection: determine the behaviour of, disable, enable, modify the behaviour of] the functions [assignment: _list of functions_] to [assignment: _the authorized identified roles_].

operations.text: determine the behaviour of, disable, enable, modify the behaviour of
determine the behaviour of, disable, enable, modify the behaviour of

activities.title: TSS Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the TSS describes:

1. The complete list of security functions whose behavior can be determined, disabled, enabled, or modified, and for each function:
.. The specific behaviors that can be controlled
.. The authorized roles permitted to perform each management operation
.. Any restrictions or conditions on management operations

1. How the TSF enforces role-based restrictions on management operations, including:
.. The mechanism used to verify role membership before permitting operations
.. The binding between authenticated identities and their assigned roles
.. How role checks are performed for cloud-native identity providers when external identity integration (FIA_UID_EXT.1) is used

1. For cloud deployments specifically:
.. How management functions are exposed via cloud interfaces (API endpoints, management consoles, CLI tools)
.. Any differences in management capabilities between local and remote administrative interfaces
.. How the TSF prevents privilege escalation through management interfaces

1. The relationship between FMT_MOF.1 functions and those specified in FMT_SMF.1/Cloud, demonstrating that all cloud management functions are subject to appropriate access restrictions.

[[EA-FMT_MOF.1-Cloud-Guidance]]

activities.title: Guidance Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the operational guidance (AGD_OPE/AGD_PRE):

1. Identifies all security functions subject to management restrictions and documents:
.. The authorized roles for each management operation
.. Procedures for assigning roles to users
.. Any default or initial role configurations

1. Provides clear instructions for administrators on:
.. How to configure role-based restrictions for management functions
.. How to verify current role assignments and permissions
.. How to audit management function access

1. Documents cloud-specific management considerations:
.. Configuration of management functions via cloud-native interfaces (e.g., Kubernetes ConfigMaps, Terraform, cloud provider consoles)
.. Secure practices for remote management over untrusted networks
.. Integration with cloud IAM for management authorization

1. Warns administrators about security implications of:
.. Modifying default role restrictions
.. Granting administrative privileges broadly
.. Configuring management access from public networks without encryption

[[EA-FMT_MOF.1-Cloud-Test]]

activities.title: 1: Role-Based Access Enforcement for Management Functions
activities.text:
For each security function identified in the TSS:

1. Step: Authenticate as a user assigned to an authorized role for managing the function.
1. Step: Attempt to perform each permitted management operation (determine behavior, disable, enable, modify).
1. Expected Result: All operations succeed for authorized roles.
1. Step: Authenticate as a user assigned to a role NOT authorized to manage the function.
1. Step: Attempt each management operation.
1. Expected Result: All operations are denied with appropriate error messages.

activities.title: 2: Cloud IAM Integration for Management Authorization
activities.text:
If the TOE integrates with external identity providers:

1. Step: Configure a cloud IAM role mapping to a TOE role with management privileges.
1. Step: Authenticate using the cloud IAM identity.
1. Step: Verify that management operations permitted for the mapped role succeed.
1. Step: Modify the cloud IAM role mapping to remove management privileges.
1. Step: Re-authenticate using the same cloud IAM identity.
1. Step: Verify that management operations are now denied.

activities.title: 3: Management Function Restriction in Distributed Deployments
activities.text:
For clustered or distributed DBMS deployments:

1. Step: Verify that management restrictions are consistently enforced across all cluster nodes.
1. Step: Attempt management operations via different nodes in the cluster.
1. Expected Result: Role-based restrictions are identical regardless of which node handles the request.
1. Step: If the cluster includes separate management and data plane components, verify restrictions apply to management plane access.

activities.title: 4: API and CLI Management Interface Restrictions
activities.text:
1. Step: Enumerate all management APIs and CLI commands exposed by the TOE.
1. Step: For each interface, attempt operations requiring elevated privileges using both authorized and unauthorized credentials.
1. Expected Result: Access controls are consistently enforced across all interfaces.

requirements.title: Cloud Configuration State Protection

elements.text: The TSF shall manage TSF data obtained from or written to cloud-native configuration and secret management systems [assignment: _list of supported cloud secret management systems_] and protect that TSF data from unauthorized disclosure and modification while it is under TOE control.

operations.text: _list of supported cloud secret management systems_

elements.text: The TSF shall obtain TSF data from, and write TSF data to, cloud-native configuration and secret management systems using a channel protected by FDP_DIT_EXT.1.

operations.text: _list of supported cloud secret management systems_

activities.title: TSS Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the TSS describes:

1. The complete list of TSF data managed by this requirement, including:
.. Database credentials and service account keys
.. Encryption keys and key material references
.. Configuration parameters affecting security functions
.. Authentication tokens and session data

1. For each operation (query, modify, delete, clear, and any other specified operations):
.. The authorized roles permitted to perform the operation
.. Any constraints or conditions on the operation
.. Audit records generated for the operation

1. The cloud-native configuration and secret management systems supported by the TOE, such as:
.. AWS Secrets Manager
.. Azure Key Vault
.. Google Cloud Secret Manager
.. HashiCorp Vault
.. Kubernetes Secrets
.. Environment variables

1. That retrieval of TSF data from, and writes of TSF data to, each supported networked system use a channel protected by the inherited FDP_DIT_EXT.1, per FMT_MTD_EXT.1.2, identifying the corresponding TLS components claimed in the ST. TLS protocol behavior itself is evaluated under the DBMS Crypto Module SD and the TLS Functional Package.

1. For each supported cloud secret management system:
.. The authentication mechanism used by the TOE to access the service (e.g., IAM roles, service principals, API keys)
.. How secrets are retrieved and protected in memory after retrieval
.. Support for secret rotation and the TOE's behavior during rotation
.. Protection of credentials used to access the secret management service itself

1. Protection of environment variables containing sensitive data:
.. How environment variables are isolated from unauthorized access
.. Protection against environment variable disclosure through error messages or logs
.. Handling of sensitive environment variables in container orchestration environments

[[EA-FMT_MTD-Cloud-Guidance]]

activities.title: Guidance Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the operational guidance (AGD_OPE/AGD_PRE):

1. Provides instructions for configuring integration with each supported cloud secret management system, including:
.. Required IAM permissions or service principal configurations
.. Network connectivity requirements
.. TLS/mTLS configuration for secret retrieval

1. Documents secure practices for credential rotation:
.. Procedures for rotating credentials without service interruption
.. How to configure automatic rotation intervals
.. Verification steps after credential rotation

1. Describes secure handling of environment variables:
.. Recommendations for passing secrets via secret management services rather than environment variables
.. Warnings about environment variable exposure risks in container environments
.. Secure configuration of orchestration platforms (Kubernetes, Docker, etc.)

1. Documents the protection of credentials in memory:
.. Any memory protection mechanisms employed
.. Guidance on minimizing credential lifetime in memory
.. Configuration options for credential caching

1. Warns administrators about common misconfigurations:
.. Storing credentials in plaintext configuration files
.. Exposing credentials in logs or error messages
.. Overly permissive IAM policies for secret access

[[EA-FMT_MTD-Cloud-Test]]

activities.title: 1: Role-Based Access to TSF Data
activities.text:
For each type of TSF data and each operation (query, modify, delete, clear):

1. Step: Authenticate as a user with an authorized role for the operation.
1. Step: Perform the operation.
1. Expected Result: Operation succeeds.
1. Step: Authenticate as a user without authorization for the operation.
1. Step: Attempt the same operation.
1. Expected Result: Operation is denied.

activities.title: 2: AWS Secrets Manager Integration
activities.text:
If AWS Secrets Manager is supported:

1. Step: Configure the TOE to retrieve database credentials from AWS Secrets Manager.
1. Step: Verify the TOE authenticates to Secrets Manager using IAM role assumption.
1. Step: Verify the TOE successfully retrieves and uses the secret.
1. Step: Rotate the secret in AWS Secrets Manager.
1. Step: Verify the TOE retrieves and uses the new secret value (either automatically or after configured refresh).
1. Step: Revoke the TOE's IAM permissions to access the secret.
1. Step: Verify the TOE handles the access denial securely (fails closed, generates appropriate audit record).

activities.title: 3: Azure Key Vault Integration
activities.text:
If Azure Key Vault is supported:

1. Step: Configure the TOE to retrieve secrets from Azure Key Vault.
1. Step: Verify the TOE authenticates using managed identity or service principal.
1. Step: Verify successful secret retrieval and usage.
1. Step: Rotate the secret in Azure Key Vault.
1. Step: Verify the TOE retrieves the rotated secret.
1. Step: Remove the TOE's access policy from Key Vault.
1. Step: Verify secure failure behavior.

activities.title: 4: HashiCorp Vault Integration
activities.text:
If HashiCorp Vault is supported:

1. Step: Configure the TOE to retrieve secrets from Vault using the appropriate auth method (e.g., Kubernetes auth, AppRole).
1. Step: Verify successful authentication and secret retrieval.
1. Step: Test secret renewal and rotation scenarios.
1. Step: Revoke the TOE's Vault token.
1. Step: Verify secure failure behavior.

activities.title: 5: Environment Variable Protection
activities.text:
1. Step: Configure sensitive data to be passed via environment variables.
1. Step: Verify the TOE does not log or expose environment variable values in error messages.
1. Step: Attempt to retrieve environment variable values through the TOE's interfaces (APIs, logs, debug endpoints).
1. Expected Result: Sensitive environment variable values are not exposed.
1. Step: For containerized deployments, verify environment variables are not exposed via container inspection commands to unauthorized users.

activities.title: 6: Credential Memory Protection
activities.text:
1. Step: Configure the TOE to retrieve credentials from a cloud secret service.
1. Step: If memory inspection capabilities are available (e.g., core dump analysis), verify credentials are not retained in memory longer than necessary.
1. Step: Verify credentials are cleared from memory when the connection is terminated or credentials are rotated.

activities.title: 7: Secret Retrieval Security
activities.text:
For each supported cloud secret management system:

1. Configure the TOE to retrieve a secret (e.g., database connection string or master key) from the cloud service.
1. Verify the retrieval request is made over a secure channel (for example, HTTPS over TLS).
1. Verify the secret is not logged in plaintext by the TOE upon retrieval.
1. If the retrieved secret is a cryptographic key (and the ST claims FCS_CKM_EXT.1 in the Crypto Module), verify that the TOE handles the key material according to the Crypto Module's FCS_CKM.6 key destruction claims and does not persist it to non-volatile storage in plaintext.

Expected result: Secrets are retrieved securely and handled appropriately in memory.

Application Note: General retrieval of configuration values, credentials, or non-key secrets from a cloud secret management service is evaluated as Cloud Module FMT_MTD_EXT.1 behavior with the retrieval channel protected by FDP_DIT_EXT.1. If the retrieved value is a Master Key imported from an external entity under the Crypto Module BYOK path, FCS_CKM_EXT.1.1 selects "Imported from External Entity", which triggers FDP_ITC_EXT.1 and mandatory mutual TLS. HTTPS/TLS secret retrieval under FDP_DIT_EXT.1 does not by itself satisfy the Crypto Module Master Key import requirement.

1. Step: Configure the TOE to retrieve secrets from Vault using the appropriate auth method (e.g., Kubernetes auth, AppRole).
1. Step: Verify successful authentication and secret retrieval.
1. Step: Test secret renewal and rotation scenarios.
1. Step: Revoke the TOE's Vault token.
1. Step: Verify secure failure behavior.

activities.title: 5: Environment Variable Protection
activities.text:
1. Step: Configure sensitive data to be passed via environment variables.
1. Step: Verify the TOE does not log or expose environment variable values in error messages.
1. Step: Attempt to retrieve environment variable values through the TOE's interfaces (APIs, logs, debug endpoints).
1. Expected Result: Sensitive environment variable values are not exposed.
1. Step: For containerized deployments, verify environment variables are not exposed via container inspection commands to unauthorized users.

activities.title: 6: Credential Memory Protection
activities.text:
1. Step: Configure the TOE to retrieve credentials from a cloud secret service.
1. Step: If memory inspection capabilities are available (e.g., core dump analysis), verify credentials are not retained in memory longer than necessary.
1. Step: Verify credentials are cleared from memory when the connection is terminated or credentials are rotated.

activities.title: 7: Secret Retrieval Security
activities.text:
For each supported cloud secret management system:

1. Configure the TOE to retrieve a secret (e.g., database connection string or master key) from the cloud service.
1. Verify the retrieval request is made over a secure channel (for example, HTTPS over TLS).
1. Verify the secret is not logged in plaintext by the TOE upon retrieval.
1. If the retrieved secret is a cryptographic key (and the ST claims FCS_CKM_EXT.1 in the Crypto Module), verify that the TOE handles the key material according to the Crypto Module's FCS_CKM.6 key destruction claims and does not persist it to non-volatile storage in plaintext.

Expected result: Secrets are retrieved securely and handled appropriately in memory.

Application Note: General retrieval of configuration values, credentials, or non-key secrets from a cloud secret management service is evaluated as Cloud Module FMT_MTD_EXT.1 behavior with the retrieval channel protected by FDP_DIT_EXT.1. If the retrieved value is a Master Key imported from an external entity under the Crypto Module BYOK path, FCS_CKM_EXT.1.1 selects "Imported from External Entity", which triggers FDP_ITC_EXT.1 and mandatory mutual TLS. HTTPS/TLS secret retrieval under FDP_DIT_EXT.1 does not by itself satisfy the Crypto Module Master Key import requirement.

requirements.searchText:
fmt_mtd.1/cloud management of tsf data (cloud) cloud mandatory the tsf shall restrict the ability to [selection: query, modify, delete, clear, [assignment: _other operations_]] the [assignment: _list of tsf data_] to [assignment: _the authorized identified roles_]. query, modify, delete, clear, [assignment: _other operations_] _list of tsf data_ _the authorized identified roles_  tss activities the evaluator shall verify that the tss describes:

1. the complete list of tsf data managed by this requirement, including:
.. database credentials and service account keys
.. encryption keys and key material references
.. configuration parameters affecting security functions
.. authentication tokens and session data

1. for each operation (query, modify, delete, clear, and any other specified operations):
.. the authorized roles permitted to perform the operation
.. any constraints or conditions on the operation
.. audit records generated for the operation

1. the cloud-native configuration and secret management systems supported by the toe, such as:
.. aws secrets manager
.. azure key vault
.. google cloud secret manager
.. hashicorp vault
.. kubernetes secrets
.. environment variables

1. that retrieval of tsf data from, and writes of tsf data to, each supported networked system use a channel protected by the inherited fdp_dit_ext.1, per fmt_mtd_ext.1.2, identifying the corresponding tls components claimed in the st. tls protocol behavior itself is evaluated under the dbms crypto module sd and the tls functional package.

1. for each supported cloud secret management system:
.. the authentication mechanism used by the toe to access the service (e.g., iam roles, service principals, api keys)
.. how secrets are retrieved and protected in memory after retrieval
.. support for secret rotation and the toe's behavior during rotation
.. protection of credentials used to access the secret management service itself

1. protection of environment variables containing sensitive data:
.. how environment variables are isolated from unauthorized access
.. protection against environment variable disclosure through error messages or logs
.. handling of sensitive environment variables in container orchestration environments

[[ea-fmt_mtd-cloud-guidance]] guidance activities the evaluator shall verify that the operational guidance (agd_ope/agd_pre):

1. provides instructions for configuring integration with each supported cloud secret management system, including:
.. required iam permissions or service principal configurations
.. network connectivity requirements
.. tls/mtls configuration for secret retrieval

1. documents secure practices for credential rotation:
.. procedures for rotating credentials without service interruption
.. how to configure automatic rotation intervals
.. verification steps after credential rotation

1. describes secure handling of environment variables:
.. recommendations for passing secrets via secret management services rather than environment variables
.. warnings about environment variable exposure risks in container environments
.. secure configuration of orchestration platforms (kubernetes, docker, etc.)

1. documents the protection of credentials in memory:
.. any memory protection mechanisms employed
.. guidance on minimizing credential lifetime in memory
.. configuration options for credential caching

1. warns administrators about common misconfigurations:
.. storing credentials in plaintext configuration files
.. exposing credentials in logs or error messages
.. overly permissive iam policies for secret access

[[ea-fmt_mtd-cloud-test]] 1: role-based access to tsf data for each type of tsf data and each operation (query, modify, delete, clear):

1. step: authenticate as a user with an authorized role for the operation.
1. step: perform the operation.
1. expected result: operation succeeds.
1. step: authenticate as a user without authorization for the operation.
1. step: attempt the same operation.
1. expected result: operation is denied. 2: aws secrets manager integration if aws secrets manager is supported:

1. step: configure the toe to retrieve database credentials from aws secrets manager.
1. step: verify the toe authenticates to secrets manager using iam role assumption.
1. step: verify the toe successfully retrieves and uses the secret.
1. step: rotate the secret in aws secrets manager.
1. step: verify the toe retrieves and uses the new secret value (either automatically or after configured refresh).
1. step: revoke the toe's iam permissions to access the secret.
1. step: verify the toe handles the access denial securely (fails closed, generates appropriate audit record). 3: azure key vault integration if azure key vault is supported:

1. step: configure the toe to retrieve secrets from azure key vault.
1. step: verify the toe authenticates using managed identity or service principal.
1. step: verify successful secret retrieval and usage.
1. step: rotate the secret in azure key vault.
1. step: verify the toe retrieves the rotated secret.
1. step: remove the toe's access policy from key vault.
1. step: verify secure failure behavior. 4: hashicorp vault integration if hashicorp vault is supported:

1. step: configure the toe to retrieve secrets from vault using the appropriate auth method (e.g., kubernetes auth, approle).
1. step: verify successful authentication and secret retrieval.
1. step: test secret renewal and rotation scenarios.

1. step: if memory inspection capabilities are available (e.g., core dump analysis), verify credentials are not retained in memory longer than necessary.
1. step: verify credentials are cleared from memory when the connection is terminated or credentials are rotated. 7: secret retrieval security for each supported cloud secret management system:

1. configure the toe to retrieve a secret (e.g., database connection string or master key) from the cloud service.
1. verify the retrieval request is made over a secure channel (for example, https over tls).
1. verify the secret is not logged in plaintext by the toe upon retrieval.
1. if the retrieved secret is a cryptographic key (and the st claims fcs_ckm_ext.1 in the crypto module), verify that the toe handles the key material according to the crypto module's fcs_ckm.6 key destruction claims and does not persist it to non-volatile storage in plaintext.

expected result: secrets are retrieved securely and handled appropriately in memory.

requirements.title: Specification of Management Functions (Cloud)

activities.title: TSS Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the TSS describes each management function listed in FMT_SMF.1.1/Cloud, specifically:

1. *Configuration of audit selection criteria (FAU_SEL.1/Cloud):*
.. The interface(s) used to configure audit selection
.. The criteria available for selection (object identity, user identity, tenant identifier, etc.)
.. How selections are persisted across TOE restart

1. *Configuration of audit export destinations (FAU_STG_EXT.1):*
.. Supported audit export destinations (CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, Splunk, etc.)
.. Configuration parameters for each destination
.. How export credentials are protected

1. *Configuration of secure external communications (FDP_DIT_EXT.1):*
.. TLS configuration parameters, including HTTPS over TLS where applicable
.. Certificate management interfaces
.. Supported cipher suites and protocol versions

1. *Management of external identity provider mappings (FIA_UID_EXT.1):*
.. How identity providers are registered with the TOE
.. How identity mappings to internal roles are configured
.. Testing and validation of identity provider configurations

1. *Monitoring and querying trusted update status and claimed integrity status:*
.. Interfaces for querying trusted update status and any claimed runtime, startup, or deployment artifact integrity status
.. Information provided in status reports
.. Actions available when integrity issues are detected

1. *Rotation of cloud-managed credentials or secrets:*
.. Interfaces for initiating credential rotation
.. Automated rotation support
.. Behavior during credential rotation

1. *Other cloud-specific management functions:*
.. Any additional functions specified by the ST author

[[EA-FMT_SMF.1-Cloud-Guidance]]

activities.title: Guidance Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the operational guidance (AGD_OPE/AGD_PRE):

1. Documents the interface(s) through which each management function is accessed, including:
.. Administrative console or dashboard
.. API endpoints (REST, gRPC, etc.)
.. CLI commands
.. Configuration files

1. Provides step-by-step instructions for performing each management function.

1. Documents any prerequisites or dependencies for management functions (e.g., TLS must be configured before enabling HTTPS for management APIs).

1. Describes the impact of each management function on TOE security and operation.

1. Documents cloud-specific management considerations:
.. Configuration via infrastructure-as-code tools (Terraform, CloudFormation, ARM templates)
.. Kubernetes ConfigMap and Secret management
.. Cloud provider-specific management interfaces

[[EA-FMT_SMF.1-Cloud-Test]]

activities.title: 1: Audit Selection Configuration
activities.text:
1. Step: Access the audit selection configuration interface.
1. Step: Configure audit selection based on each available criterion (user identity, tenant identifier, event type, etc.).
1. Step: Generate events matching and not matching the selection criteria.
1. Expected Result: Only events matching the criteria are audited.
1. Step: Restart the TOE.
1. Step: Verify audit selection configuration persists.

activities.title: 2: Audit Export Configuration
activities.text:
1. Step: Configure audit export to each supported destination.
1. Step: Generate auditable events.
1. Step: Verify events are exported to the configured destination.
1. Step: Misconfigure the export destination (invalid credentials, unreachable endpoint).
1. Step: Verify the TOE handles export failures appropriately (generates local alerts, retains events locally).

activities.title: 3: External Identity Provider Management
activities.text:
1. Step: Register an external identity provider (cloud IAM, LDAP, OIDC).
1. Step: Configure identity-to-role mappings.
1. Step: Test authentication using the external provider.
1. Step: Modify the mappings.
1. Step: Verify the modified mappings take effect.
1. Step: Remove the identity provider configuration.
1. Step: Verify authentication via that provider is no longer possible.

activities.title: 4: Trusted Update and Integrity Status Querying
activities.text:
1. Step: Query trusted update status using available interfaces.
1. Step: Verify the status report includes relevant update information.
1. Step: If FPT_TST.1 or FPT_SBT_EXT.1 is implemented, trigger an integrity check and verify the status is updated.

activities.title: 5: Credential Rotation
activities.text:
1. Step: Initiate credential rotation through the management interface.
1. Step: Verify old credentials are invalidated.
1. Step: Verify new credentials are functional.
1. Step: If automated rotation is supported, configure automatic rotation and verify it executes correctly.

activities.title: 6: Management Function Access via Cloud-Native Interfaces
activities.text:
For containerized deployments:

1. Step: Verify management functions can be configured via Kubernetes ConfigMaps or Helm values.
1. Step: Apply configuration changes using kubectl apply or helm upgrade.
1. Step: Verify the TOE applies the new configuration.

For cloud-deployed TOEs:

1. Step: Verify management functions can be configured via cloud provider interfaces (parameter groups, configuration services).
1. Step: Apply changes via the cloud provider console or CLI.
1. Step: Verify the TOE applies the new configuration.

requirements.title: Security Roles (Cloud)

elements.text: The TSF shall maintain the roles [assignment: _the authorized identified roles (e.g., tenant administrator, tenant user, cloud audit authority)_].

operations.text: _the authorized identified roles (e.g., tenant administrator, tenant user, cloud audit authority)_

elements.text: The TSF shall be able to associate users with roles.

operations.text: _the authorized identified roles (e.g., tenant administrator, tenant user, cloud audit authority)_

activities.title: TSS Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the TSS describes:

1. The complete list of roles maintained by the TSF, as assigned in FMT_SMR.1.1/Cloud by the ST author. The SFR's application note gives tenant administrator, tenant user, and cloud audit authority (a read-only role for audit data and security status) as examples; this SD does not levy roles beyond the ST's assignment.

1. For each role:
.. The privileges and capabilities associated with the role
.. How the role is differentiated from other roles
.. Any hierarchical relationships between roles

1. The mechanism for associating users with roles:
.. Direct role assignment within the TOE
.. Role mapping from external identity providers
.. Default role assignments for new users

1. How role membership is verified during access control decisions.

1. For cloud deployments specifically:
.. How roles are mapped from cloud IAM roles or groups
.. Multi-tenant role isolation (how roles in one tenant cannot affect another tenant)
.. Service account and machine identity role assignments

[[EA-FMT_SMR.1-Cloud-Guidance]]

activities.title: Guidance Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the operational guidance (AGD_OPE/AGD_PRE):

1. Documents all pre-defined roles and their associated privileges.

1. Provides instructions for:
.. Creating custom roles (if supported)
.. Assigning users to roles
.. Removing users from roles
.. Configuring role mappings from external identity providers

1. Documents the minimum privileges required for each administrative task.

1. Provides guidance on implementing least-privilege principles using the TOE's role model.

1. Documents cloud-specific role management:
.. Mapping cloud IAM roles to TOE roles
.. Managing roles in multi-tenant deployments
.. Role inheritance and hierarchy (if applicable)

[[EA-FMT_SMR.1-Cloud-Test]]

activities.title: 1: Role Maintenance
activities.text:
1. Step: Verify the TSF maintains each role specified in the TSS.
1. Step: Query the list of roles using available administrative interfaces.
1. Expected Result: All specified roles are present.

Note: Tests 2 through 4 exercise the roles assigned in the ST, using the example role names for readability. Where the ST does not assign a role corresponding to the one named, the evaluator shall perform the equivalent privilege-separation verification using the roles the ST does assign, and shall omit a test only when no assigned role provides the corresponding capability.

activities.title: 2: User-Role Association
activities.text:
1. Step: Create a new user (or use an existing user not assigned to any role).
1. Step: Associate the user with the Tenant Administrator role.
1. Step: Verify the user can perform administrative functions.
1. Step: Remove the user from the Tenant Administrator role.
1. Step: Verify the user can no longer perform administrative functions.

activities.title: 3: Tenant User Role Verification
activities.text:
1. Step: Associate a user with the Tenant User role.
1. Step: Verify the user can perform standard database operations (queries, data modification as permitted).
1. Step: Verify the user cannot perform administrative operations.

activities.title: 4: Cloud Audit Authority Role Verification
activities.text:
1. Step: Associate a user with the Cloud Audit Authority role.
1. Step: Verify the user can access audit data and security status information.
1. Step: Verify the user cannot modify security configurations or data.
1. Step: Verify the user cannot modify audit records.

activities.title: 5: Cloud IAM Role Mapping
activities.text:
If external identity provider integration is used:

1. Step: Configure a cloud IAM role (e.g., AWS IAM role, Azure AD group) to map to the Tenant Administrator role.
1. Step: Authenticate using an identity assigned the cloud IAM role.
1. Step: Verify the user has Tenant Administrator privileges.
1. Step: Change the IAM role mapping to Tenant User.
1. Step: Re-authenticate using the same identity.
1. Step: Verify the user now has only Tenant User privileges.

activities.title: 6: Multi-Tenant Role Isolation
activities.text:
For multi-tenant deployments:

1. Step: Create roles with the same name in two different tenants.
1. Step: Assign different users to each role.
1. Step: Verify that users in Tenant A cannot access resources in Tenant B, even with the same role name.
1. Step: Verify administrative actions in one tenant do not affect another tenant.

activities.title: 7: Role Assignment/Removal Audit
activities.text:
1. Step: Assign a user to a role.
1. Step: Verify an audit record is generated for the assignment.
1. Step: Remove the user from the role.
1. Step: Verify an audit record is generated for the removal.
1. Step: Verify audit records include user identity, role, action, and timestamp.

requirements.title: Availability and Resilience Signaling

elements.text: The TSF shall monitor its runtime environment for indicators of [selection: configuration drift, resource unavailability, integrity failure via self-test, orchestration failure, [assignment: _other monitored conditions_]].

operations.text: configuration drift, resource unavailability, integrity failure via self-test, orchestration failure, [assignment: _other monitored conditions_]

elements.text: The TSF shall notify [assignment: _authorized administrator, orchestrator, or monitoring system_] upon detection of a monitored condition.

operations.text: configuration drift, resource unavailability, integrity failure via self-test, orchestration failure, [assignment: _other monitored conditions_]

activities.title: TSS Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the TSS describes:

1. The runtime environment conditions monitored by the TSF, from the selection:
.. *Configuration drift*: Changes to security-relevant configuration from expected baseline
.. *Resource unavailability*: Failure of required resources (storage, network, services)
.. *Integrity failure via self-test*: Detection of integrity violations (requires FPT_TST.1)
.. *Orchestration failure*: Failures in container orchestration or deployment systems
.. *Other monitored conditions*: Any additional conditions specified by the ST author

1. For each monitored condition:
.. The specific indicators or metrics used to detect the condition
.. The monitoring frequency or trigger events
.. The threshold or criteria for determining a condition exists
.. The response actions taken upon detection

1. The notification mechanisms and recipients:
.. Supported notification methods (alerts, logs, API callbacks, cloud-native monitoring)
.. How notification recipients are configured
.. The information included in notifications

1. For cloud deployments specifically:
.. Integration with cloud-native monitoring services (CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, etc.)
.. Kubernetes health checks and liveness/readiness probes
.. Container orchestration failure detection

[[EA-FPT_ARS_EXT.1-Cloud-Guidance]]

activities.title: Guidance Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the operational guidance (AGD_OPE/AGD_PRE):

1. Documents the configuration of each monitored condition:
.. How to enable/disable monitoring for specific conditions
.. How to configure detection thresholds
.. How to define expected baseline configurations for drift detection

1. Provides instructions for configuring notifications:
.. Supported notification destinations
.. Configuration of cloud-native monitoring integration
.. Alerting rules and escalation procedures

1. Documents recommended responses to each type of detected condition.

1. Provides guidance for cloud-specific monitoring:
.. Integration with container orchestration health checks
.. Configuration of cloud provider monitoring services
.. Best practices for alerting in ephemeral/elastic environments

[[EA-FPT_ARS_EXT.1-Cloud-Test]]

activities.title: 1: Configuration Drift Detection
activities.text:
If configuration drift monitoring is selected:

1. Step: Establish a known security configuration baseline.
1. Step: Modify a security-relevant configuration parameter outside of authorized management interfaces (e.g., directly edit configuration file, modify database system table).
1. Expected Result: TSF detects the drift and generates a notification.
1. Step: Verify the notification includes information about what changed.

activities.title: 2: Resource Unavailability Detection
activities.text:
If resource unavailability monitoring is selected:

1. Step: Identify a resource the TOE depends on (storage volume, network service, cloud service).
1. Step: Make the resource unavailable (disconnect network, unmount storage, stop service).
1. Expected Result: TSF detects the unavailability and generates a notification.
1. Step: Restore the resource.
1. Step: Verify the TSF detects recovery (if recovery notification is supported).

activities.title: 3: Integrity Failure Detection via Self-Test
activities.text:
If integrity failure via self-test is selected (requires FPT_TST.1):

1. Step: Trigger a self-test that will fail (if testing capability exists, e.g., corrupt a test file).
1. Expected Result: TSF detects the integrity failure and generates a notification.
1. Step: Verify the notification identifies the integrity failure.

activities.title: 4: Orchestration Failure Detection
activities.text:
If orchestration failure monitoring is selected:

For Kubernetes deployments:

1. Step: Deploy the TOE as a Kubernetes pod with liveness and readiness probes.
1. Step: Verify probes respond correctly under normal operation.
1. Step: Simulate a condition that should cause probe failure.
1. Expected Result: TSF indicates failure via probe response.
1. Step: Verify Kubernetes takes configured action (restart pod, mark unhealthy).

For other orchestration platforms:

1. Step: Simulate an orchestration failure (deployment timeout, resource allocation failure).
1. Expected Result: TSF detects and signals the failure.

activities.title: 5: Notification Mechanism Verification
activities.text:
For each configured notification recipient:

1. Step: Trigger a monitored condition.
1. Step: Verify the notification is received by the configured recipient.
1. Step: Verify the notification contains required information:
.. Condition type
.. Timestamp
.. Affected component or resource
.. Severity (if applicable)

activities.title: 6: Cloud-Native Monitoring Integration
activities.text:
If cloud-native monitoring integration is supported:

1. Step: Configure the TOE to send alerts to CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, or equivalent.
1. Step: Trigger a monitored condition.
1. Step: Verify the alert appears in the cloud monitoring service.
1. Step: Verify alert metadata is correct.

requirements.title: Failure with Preservation of Secure State

elements.text: The TSF shall preserve a secure state when the following types of failures occur: [assignment: _list of types of failures in the TSF_].

operations.text: _list of types of failures in the TSF_
_list of types of failures in the TSF_

activities.title: TSS Activities
Guidance Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify the operational guidance describes any configuration that affects failure-handling behavior, and the procedures for recovering the TOE to normal operation from the secure state.

[[EA-FPT_FLS.1-Cloud-Test]]

activities.title: 1: Secure State Preservation
activities.text:
For each failure type claimed in the ST (or, where a failure type cannot practically be induced in the test environment, for a representative subset with the remainder verified through simulation or provider-supported demonstration recorded in the ETR):

1. Induce or simulate the failure according to the TSS description (for example, revoking connectivity to a required cloud service, detaching storage, or terminating an instance through the orchestrator).
1. Verify the TOE enters the secure state defined in the TSS.
1. While the TOE is in the secure state, attempt to access protected data or perform a security-relevant operation, and verify the attempt is refused consistent with the TSS definition of the secure state.
1. Recover the TOE per the operational guidance and verify normal operation resumes.

Expected result: For each claimed failure type, the TOE preserves the TSS-defined secure state and protected data is not exposed during the failure condition.

requirements.searchText:
fpt_fls.1 failure with preservation of secure state cloud mandatory the tsf shall preserve a secure state when the following types of failures occur: [assignment: _list of types of failures in the tsf_]. _list of types of failures in the tsf_  tss activities the evaluator shall verify the tss:

- identifies each failure type claimed in the fpt_fls.1.1 assignment;
- defines what constitutes a secure state for the toe (for example, refusing new connections, denying access to protected data, or controlled shutdown), including whether the secure state differs by failure type;
- describes, for each claimed failure type, how the toe detects or is informed of the failure and how it transitions to the secure state; and
- where a claimed failure type overlaps a condition monitored under fpt_ars_ext.1, describes the relationship between the notification behavior and the secure-state transition.

[[ea-fpt_fls.1-cloud-guidance]] guidance activities the evaluator shall verify the operational guidance describes any configuration that affects failure-handling behavior, and the procedures for recovering the toe to normal operation from the secure state.

[[ea-fpt_fls.1-cloud-test]] 1: secure state preservation for each failure type claimed in the st (or, where a failure type cannot practically be induced in the test environment, for a representative subset with the remainder verified through simulation or provider-supported demonstration recorded in the etr):

1. induce or simulate the failure according to the tss description (for example, revoking connectivity to a required cloud service, detaching storage, or terminating an instance through the orchestrator).
1. verify the toe enters the secure state defined in the tss.
1. while the toe is in the secure state, attempt to access protected data or perform a security-relevant operation, and verify the attempt is refused consistent with the tss definition of the secure state.
1. recover the toe per the operational guidance and verify normal operation resumes.

expected result: for each claimed failure type, the toe preserves the tss-defined secure state and protected data is not exposed during the failure condition.

requirements.title: Basic Internal TSF Data Transfer Protection

elements.text: The TSF shall protect TSF data from [selection: _disclosure, modification_] when it is transmitted between separate parts of the TOE.

activities.title: TSS Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the TSS describes:

1. The protection type selected (disclosure, modification, or both):
.. If disclosure protection is selected, the confidentiality mechanisms used
.. If modification protection is selected, the integrity mechanisms used

1. The separate parts of the TOE between which TSF data is transferred:
.. Database nodes in a cluster
.. Control plane and data plane components
.. Replication endpoints
.. Management services and database engines

1. The specific TSF data protected during transfer:
.. Authentication credentials and session tokens
.. Configuration data
.. Replication data
.. Health check and status information
.. Cryptographic keys or key material

1. The mechanisms used to protect transfers:
.. TSF-implemented cryptographic protocols (TLS, mTLS)
.. Authentication between components
.. Any supplementary operational-environment measures (network isolation, private networks, VPCs, or OS/kernel-implemented protocols such as IPsec) — the TSS shall distinguish these from TSF-provided protection, because they are defense in depth and cannot alone satisfy FPT_ITT.1 (see the PP-Module's Security Function Allocation)

1. For cloud deployments specifically:
.. Protection of cluster communication in cloud networks
.. Use of cloud-native encryption for inter-component traffic
.. Network security groups or policies for component isolation

[[EA-FPT_ITT.1-Cloud-Guidance]]

activities.title: Guidance Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the operational guidance (AGD_OPE/AGD_PRE):

1. Documents the deployment architecture for distributed configurations.

1. Provides instructions for:
.. Configuring encryption for inter-node communication
.. Setting up mutual authentication between components
.. Configuring network isolation for cluster traffic

1. Documents the cryptographic algorithms used for protection (reference to DBMS Crypto Module if applicable).

1. Provides cloud-specific deployment guidance:
.. Kubernetes network policies for pod-to-pod encryption
.. Cloud VPC configuration for cluster isolation
.. Load balancer and service mesh configuration

[[EA-FPT_ITT.1-Cloud-Test]]

activities.title: 1: Disclosure Protection Verification
activities.text:
If disclosure protection is selected:

1. Step: Deploy the TOE in a distributed configuration (multi-node cluster).
1. Step: Capture network traffic between TOE components.
1. Step: Generate traffic that includes TSF data (authentication, configuration synchronization).
1. Expected Result: Captured traffic is encrypted; TSF data is not visible in plaintext.

activities.title: 2: Modification Protection Verification
activities.text:
If modification protection is selected:

1. Step: Deploy the TOE in a distributed configuration.
1. Step: If possible, intercept and modify traffic between components (man-in-the-middle).
1. Expected Result: Receiving component detects modification and rejects the data.
1. Step: Verify an appropriate error or audit record is generated.

activities.title: 3: Cluster Communication Security
activities.text:
For clustered deployments:

1. Step: Verify all nodes in the cluster use protected communication channels.
1. Step: Attempt to add a rogue node to the cluster without proper credentials.
1. Expected Result: The rogue node is rejected.
1. Step: Verify legitimate cluster operations succeed with protection enabled.

activities.title: 4: Management/Data Plane Separation
activities.text:
If the TOE has separate management and data planes:

1. Step: Verify management plane traffic is protected independently of data plane traffic.
1. Step: Capture traffic on both planes.
1. Expected Result: Both planes use appropriate protection mechanisms.

activities.title: 5: Cloud Network Isolation (Supplementary — Operational Environment)
activities.text:
For cloud deployments. This test examines defense-in-depth configuration of the operational environment and does not contribute TSF evidence for FPT_ITT.1; the TSF evidence is provided by Tests 1 through 4.

1. Step: Verify cluster nodes communicate only over private networks/VPCs.
1. Step: Verify external access to inter-node communication is blocked.
1. Step: For Kubernetes deployments, verify network policies restrict inter-pod communication appropriately.

activities.title: 6: Replication Traffic Protection
activities.text:
For replicated deployments:

1. Step: Configure replication between primary and replica instances.
1. Step: Capture replication traffic.
1. Expected Result: Replication data is protected according to selected protection type.
1. Step: Verify replication functions correctly with protection enabled.

requirements.title: Trusted Update

elements.text: The TSF shall provide authorized users the ability to query the current version of the TOE software/firmware.

The TSF shall provide a mechanism to apply updates using [selection: cloud-native deployment services, trusted image registries, secure boot or image verification mechanisms as defined in FPT_SBT_EXT.1, [assignment: _other trusted update mechanisms_]].

operations.text: cloud-native deployment services, trusted image registries, secure boot or image verification mechanisms as defined in FPT_SBT_EXT.1, [assignment: _other trusted update mechanisms_]

elements.text: The TSF shall verify the authenticity and integrity of updates prior to installation using [selection: _digital signature verification using FCS_COP.1/SigVer as claimed in the DBMS Crypto Module_, _cryptographic hash verification against a reference value that is obtained via a channel protected by FDP_DIT_EXT.1 or held in storage protected from unauthorized modification, as identified in the TSS_].

operations.text: _digital signature verification using FCS_COP.1/SigVer as claimed in the DBMS Crypto Module_, _cryptographic hash verification against a reference value that is obtained via a channel protected by FDP_DIT_EXT.1 or held in storage protected from unauthorized modification, as identified in the TSS_
cloud-native deployment services, trusted image registries, secure boot or image verification mechanisms as defined in FPT_SBT_EXT.1, [assignment: _other trusted update mechanisms_]

_digital signature verification using FCS_COP.1/SigVer as claimed in the DBMS Crypto Module_, _cryptographic hash verification against a reference value that is obtained via a channel protected by FDP_DIT_EXT.1 or held in storage protected from unauthorized modification, as identified in the TSS_

activities.title: TSS Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the TSS describes:

1. The mechanism for querying the current TOE version:
.. Interface(s) used (CLI, API, console)
.. Information provided (version number, build date, component versions)

1. The update mechanisms supported:
.. Cloud-native deployment services (AWS Systems Manager, Azure Update Management)
.. Trusted image registries (container registries with signing)
.. Secure boot or image verification mechanisms (if FPT_SBT_EXT.1 is selected)
.. Package management systems
.. Manual update procedures

1. The authenticity and integrity verification mechanisms:
.. Digital signature verification (signing authority, algorithms)
.. Cryptographic hash verification (algorithms, source of known-good values)
.. Certificate chain validation

1. For cloud deployments specifically:
.. Container image signature verification (Docker Content Trust, Sigstore, etc.)
.. Verification of images from cloud provider registries
.. Integration with cloud-native update services

[[EA-FPT_TUD_EXT.1-Cloud-Guidance]]

activities.title: Guidance Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the operational guidance (AGD_OPE/AGD_PRE):

1. Documents the procedure for querying current TOE version.

1. Provides step-by-step update procedures for each supported mechanism.

1. Documents the verification process:
.. How to verify update authenticity before installation
.. Expected signatures or hashes
.. Actions if verification fails

1. Provides guidance for cloud-native updates:
.. Container image update procedures
.. Rolling update strategies for minimal downtime
.. Rollback procedures if updates fail

1. Documents the trusted sources for updates:
.. Official repositories or registries
.. Signing keys or certificates
.. Verification endpoints

[[EA-FPT_TUD_EXT.1-Cloud-Test]]

activities.title: 1: Version Query
activities.text:
1. Step: Use each available interface to query the current TOE version.
1. Expected Result: Version information is returned accurately.
1. Step: Verify the version information matches the installed TOE version.

activities.title: 2: Authentic Update Installation
activities.text:
1. Step: Obtain a genuine update package from a trusted source.
1. Step: Apply the update using the documented procedure.
1. Step: Verify the update is verified for authenticity and integrity.
1. Step: Verify the update installs successfully.
1. Step: Query the version to confirm the update was applied.

activities.title: 3: Tampered Update Rejection
activities.text:
1. Step: Obtain a genuine update package.
1. Step: Modify the package (change a byte, alter contents).
1. Step: Attempt to apply the tampered update.
1. Expected Result: The TSF rejects the update due to integrity verification failure.
1. Step: Verify an appropriate error message or audit record is generated.

activities.title: 4: Invalid Signature Rejection
activities.text:
1. Step: Create or obtain an update package signed with an untrusted key.
1. Step: Attempt to apply the package.
1. Expected Result: The TSF rejects the update due to authenticity verification failure.
1. Step: Verify an appropriate error message or audit record is generated.

activities.title: 5: Cloud-Native Update Verification
activities.text:
For container deployments. This test contributes TSF evidence for FPT_TUD_EXT.1.3 only where the ST includes the image-verification mechanism in the TOE boundary (for example, via FPT_SBT_EXT.1 or TOE-implemented deployment verification). Where signature enforcement is performed by the registry or orchestrator in the operational environment, this test is a supplementary environment-integration check, and the TSF evidence for FPT_TUD_EXT.1.3 is provided by Tests 2 through 4.

1. Step: Push a signed container image to the registry.
1. Step: Deploy the TOE using the signed image.
1. Expected Result: Deployment succeeds.
1. Step: Push an unsigned or invalidly signed image.
1. Step: Attempt to deploy using the unsigned image.
1. Expected Result: Deployment fails or generates a security alert.

For cloud-managed update services:

1. Step: Configure the TOE to receive updates from the cloud update service.
1. Step: Apply an update through the service.
1. Step: Verify the update was validated before installation.

activities.title: 6: Update Audit
activities.text:
1. Step: Apply a successful update.
1. Step: Verify an audit record is generated indicating:
.. Previous version
.. New version
.. Timestamp
.. User or system that initiated the update

1. step: verify the update was validated before installation. 6: update audit 1. step: apply a successful update.
1. step: verify an audit record is generated indicating:
.. previous version
.. new version
.. timestamp
.. user or system that initiated the update

requirements.title: Secure Boot and Image Verification

elements.text: The TSF shall verify the integrity of its boot-time or deployment-time components using [assignment: _verification mechanisms_].

The TSF shall verify the authenticity of its boot-time or deployment-time components by validating [selection: digital signatures from trusted signing authorities, cryptographic hashes against known-good values that are obtained via a channel protected by FDP_DIT_EXT.1 or held in storage protected from unauthorized modification, as identified in the TSS].

operations.text: digital signatures from trusted signing authorities, cryptographic hashes against known-good values that are obtained via a channel protected by FDP_DIT_EXT.1 or held in storage protected from unauthorized modification, as identified in the TSS

elements.text: The TSF shall [selection: prevent boot/startup, alert administrators, [assignment: _other action_]] if integrity or authenticity verification fails.

operations.text: digital signatures from trusted signing authorities, cryptographic hashes against known-good values that are obtained via a channel protected by FDP_DIT_EXT.1 or held in storage protected from unauthorized modification, as identified in the TSS

activities.title: TSS Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the TSS describes:

1. The boot-time or deployment-time components subject to verification:
.. Executable binaries
.. Configuration files
.. Container images
.. Dependent libraries or modules

1. The integrity verification mechanisms:
.. Cryptographic hash algorithms used
.. Hash storage and comparison methods
.. Verification timing (before execution, at deployment, etc.)

1. The authenticity verification mechanisms:
.. Digital signature verification
.. Signing authorities trusted
.. Certificate or key management

1. The action taken if verification fails:
.. Prevent boot/startup
.. Alert administrators
.. Other specified actions

1. For containerized deployments:
.. Container image signature verification (Docker Content Trust, Sigstore, Cosign)
.. Registry trust configuration
.. Kubernetes admission controller integration

[[EA-FPT_SBT_EXT.1-Cloud-Guidance]]

activities.title: Guidance Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the operational guidance (AGD_OPE/AGD_PRE):

1. Documents the components verified at boot/deployment time.

1. Provides instructions for:
.. Configuring trusted signing keys or certificates
.. Setting up image registry trust
.. Configuring verification policies

1. Documents the expected behavior when verification fails.

1. Provides cloud-specific guidance:
.. Container image signing procedures
.. Kubernetes admission controller configuration
.. Cloud provider image verification services

[[EA-FPT_SBT_EXT.1-Cloud-Test]]

activities.title: 1: Boot Chain Verification
activities.text:
1. Step: Boot the TOE normally.
1. Step: Verify all boot-time integrity checks pass.
1. Step: Verify the TOE starts successfully.

activities.title: 2: Tampered Boot Component Rejection
activities.text:
1. Step: Modify a boot-time component (if possible in the test environment).
1. Step: Attempt to boot the TOE.
1. Expected Result: TOE detects integrity failure and takes configured action (prevent boot or alert).
1. Step: Verify the action matches the configuration.

activities.title: 3: Container Image Signature Verification
activities.text:
For containerized deployments:

1. Step: Deploy the TOE using a properly signed container image.
1. Expected Result: Deployment succeeds.
1. Step: Attempt to deploy using an unsigned image.
1. Expected Result: Deployment fails or generates security alert.
1. Step: Attempt to deploy using an image with invalid signature.
1. Expected Result: Deployment fails or generates security alert.

activities.title: 4: Kubernetes Admission Control Integration
activities.text:
If Kubernetes admission controllers are used. This test contributes TSF evidence for FPT_SBT_EXT.1 only where the ST includes the admission-control verification in the TOE boundary; where the admission controller is operational-environment machinery, this test is a supplementary environment-integration check and the TSF evidence is provided by the other FPT_SBT_EXT.1 tests.

1. Step: Configure an admission controller to validate image signatures.
1. Step: Attempt to deploy a pod with a signed image.
1. Expected Result: Pod is admitted.
1. Step: Attempt to deploy a pod with an unsigned image.
1. Expected Result: Pod is rejected by the admission controller.

activities.title: 5: Administrator Alert on Verification Failure
activities.text:
If alert is selected as the failure action:

1. Step: Trigger a verification failure (deploy unsigned image, modify component).
1. Step: Verify an alert is generated to administrators.
1. Step: Verify the alert includes information about the failure.

requirements.title: TSF Self-Test

elements.text: The TSF shall run a suite of self-tests [selection: during initial start-up, periodically during normal operation, at the request of the authorized user, under [assignment: _conditions under which self-test shall occur_]] to demonstrate the correct operation of [selection: [assignment: _parts of the TSF_], the TSF].

operations.text: during initial start-up, periodically during normal operation, at the request of the authorized user, under [assignment: _conditions under which self-test shall occur_]

elements.text: The TSF shall provide authorized users the capability to verify the integrity of [selection: [assignment: _parts of TSF data_], TSF data].
The TSF shall provide authorized users the capability to verify the integrity of [selection: [assignment: _parts of TSF_], TSF].

operations.text: during initial start-up, periodically during normal operation, at the request of the authorized user, under [assignment: _conditions under which self-test shall occur_]

activities.title: TSS Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the TSS describes:

1. The conditions under which self-tests are run:
.. During initial start-up
.. Periodically during normal operation (specify interval)
.. At the request of authorized user
.. Other specified conditions

1. The self-tests performed:
.. Cryptographic module integrity checks (if crypto is implemented)
.. File or image integrity validation
.. Runtime environment verification
.. Configuration integrity checks
.. Other tests as specified

1. For each self-test:
.. What is tested
.. Expected results
.. Actions on failure

1. The mechanism for users to verify TSF integrity:
.. Interfaces available
.. Information provided

[[EA-FPT_TST.1-Cloud-Guidance]]

activities.title: Guidance Activities
activities.text:
The evaluator shall verify that the operational guidance (AGD_OPE/AGD_PRE):

1. Documents all self-tests performed and their purpose.

1. Provides instructions for:
.. Configuring self-test schedules (if periodic testing is supported)
.. Initiating on-demand self-tests
.. Interpreting self-test results

1. Documents the expected behavior when self-tests fail.

1. Provides troubleshooting guidance for self-test failures.

[[EA-FPT_TST.1-Cloud-Test]]

activities.title: 1: Startup Self-Test
activities.text:
If startup self-test is selected:

1. Step: Start the TOE.
1. Step: Verify self-tests are executed during startup (check logs, audit records).
1. Step: Verify self-tests complete successfully.
1. Step: Verify the TOE becomes operational after successful tests.

activities.title: 2: Periodic Self-Test
activities.text:
If periodic self-test is selected:

1. Step: Configure the self-test interval.
1. Step: Wait for the interval to elapse.
1. Step: Verify self-tests are executed (check logs, audit records).

activities.title: 3: On-Demand Self-Test
activities.text:
If on-demand self-test is selected:

1. Step: As an authorized user, initiate a self-test.
1. Step: Verify the self-test executes.
1. Step: Verify results are reported to the user.

activities.title: 4: Self-Test Failure Detection
activities.text:
1. Step: If possible, cause a self-test to fail (corrupt a verified file, alter configuration).
1. Step: Trigger the self-test.
1. Expected Result: TSF detects the failure.
1. Step: Verify the configured failure action is taken (alert, shutdown, degraded mode).
1. Step: Verify an audit record is generated for the failure.

activities.title: 5: TSF Data Integrity Verification
activities.text:
1. Step: Request TSF data integrity verification (as authorized user).
1. Step: Verify the result indicates current integrity status.
1. Step: Modify TSF data (if possible in test environment).
1. Step: Request integrity verification again.
1. Expected Result: Verification detects the modification.

activities.title: 6: Cryptographic Self-Test
activities.text:
If cryptographic self-tests are implemented:

1. Step: Verify known-answer tests or algorithm validation tests are performed.
1. Step: Verify cryptographic operations are blocked until self-tests pass.

requirements.title: Selective Audit (Tenant Separation)

elements.text: The TSF shall be able to select the set of events to be audited from the set of all auditable events based on the following attributes: [_tenant identifier, user identifier, service role, object identifier, and event type_].

activities.title: TSS Activities
activities.text: The evaluator shall verify the TSS describes how audit event selection is scoped by tenant identifier, user identifier, service role, object identifier, and event type. Restriction of audit record queries to the owning tenant is an access-control rule of FDP_ACF.1/DBaaS and is evaluated there; Test 1 below may be performed jointly with the FDP_ACF.1/DBaaS tests.

activities.title: Guidance Activities
activities.text: The evaluator shall verify the operational guidance describes how audit selection is configured, the attributes on which selection can be based, and the roles authorized to change tenant-scoped audit selection.

activities.title: 1: Audit Scope Leakage
activities.text:
1. Generate audit events in Tenant A and Tenant B.
1. As Tenant A administrator, query the audit logs.
1. Verify that only Tenant A records are returned unless an evaluated provider, cross-tenant, or compliance function explicitly authorizes broader access.
1. Analyze the query results to ensure no Tenant B data, usernames, object names, query text, or tenant identifiers are visible to Tenant A unless explicitly authorized by the evaluated configuration.
